summary.intent: Merge main branch into historical-carrot feature branch and update the chat/graphql consumers to use the new ModelCapabilities abstraction directly instead of provider lookups.
summary.outcome: Merge succeeded cleanly with all 264 provider tests and 160 chat tests passing. Consumer-side changes were committed, exposing supports_reasoning via GraphQL and wiring chat to use capabilities directly.

repo: New Gemini 3.x models flow through catalog_to_discovered → DiscoveredModel (capabilities: None) → ModelCapabilities::infer(), so no manual capability registration is needed for new models matching existing prefix patterns.
supports_reasoning_for_model() uses id.starts_with('gemini-3') to gate reasoning support; adding new model families requires updating this function.
gemma- prefix is excluded from chat-capable models; gemini live/image variants are also excluded — these are explicit exclusion patterns in lib.rs.

code.finding: Refactored to use ModelCapabilities directly instead of provider lookups — the key payoff of the historical-carrot refactor.
Added supports_reasoning field to the GraphQL ModelInfo type.

workflow: Kept merge commit and consumer changes as separate commits rather than amending the merge commit.
Background task notifications carry an output file path that must be explicitly read to retrieve results.

friction: API 500 Internal Server Error interrupted one of the test run tool calls, requiring the result to be recovered from a background task output file.
open_items: Verify downstream consumers beyond chat/graphql are updated to use capabilities directly rather than provider lookups.
The historical-carrot branch is still a worktree — needs eventual PR/merge to main once the full refactor is complete.
